title: Prompt Engineering is Dead — Agent Engineering is the New Skill Stack
description: The X conversation has crystallised: basic prompt engineering is now table stakes. The trending meta is 'Agent Engineering' — designing robust systems that plan, use tools, reflect, verify, and loop until they succeed. Builders are stacking Loop Engineering and Harness Engineering on top of prompts to ship production-grade autonomous agents.

title: RACE, RISE, CLEAR, GROW: The Prompt Frameworks Dominating 2026
description: Structured mnemonic frameworks are seeing a massive resurgence as models get more capable. RACE (Role-Action-Context-Expectation), RISE, CLEAR, and GROW are being shared widely as reliable templates for consistent, expert-level outputs across Claude, GPT-4o, and Grok. These aren't just acronyms — they're production patterns.

title: The 5-Part Coding Agent Template Going Viral: Goal, Context, Scope, Don't Change, Verify
description: A deceptively simple 5-field template is dominating AI coding discussion. The structure — Goal, Context, Scope, Don't Change, Verify — forces clarity and prevents agent drift. Builders report it works across Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot for refactoring, debugging, and test generation.

title: Claude Code Production Prompts: Curated GitHub Collection Takes Off
description: A dedicated GitHub repository collecting production-grade Claude Code system prompts and agent templates is gaining traction fast. The collection covers refactoring workflows, test generation harnesses, and multi-file agent orchestration patterns — all battle-tested in real codebases.

title: GPT Image 2 Luxury Editorial Portrait: The Prompt Replacing $5K Photoshoots
description: A meticulously structured portrait prompt for OpenAI's GPT Image 2 is going viral for producing magazine-quality luxury editorial shots from a single selfie. The prompt chains facial preservation instructions with detailed environment descriptions, lighting specs, and lens references — consistently delivering Vogue/GQ-level results.

title: Minimalist Philosophical Poster: The Typography-First Prompt Blowing Up for Wall Art
description: A restrained, typography-first prompt template is trending for generating elegant philosophical posters suited for print and interiors. The formula uses negative space, serif/sans-serif pairings, muted palettes, and a quiet philosophical tone — producing consistently gallery-worthy compositions across multiple image models.

title: AI Filmmaking in 2026: Shot Discipline and Camera Movement Beat Prompt Stacking
description: The AI video community is converging on a clear thesis: filmmaking fundamentals outperform jargon-stuffed prompts. Top creators now use structured shot lists with explicit camera movements (push-in, dolly zoom, tracking shot), one subject per shot, and LLM-generated previs workflows — treating Kling, Runway, and Veo like camera rigs rather than magic wands.

title: Stop Writing One-Off Prompts: Why Template Engines Are the Agent Architecture of 2026
description: Top builders are abandoning static prompts in favour of dynamic template engines (Jinja2, Handlebars, structured JSON) that inject context, tools, and verification rules at runtime. This architectural shift turns fragile one-shots into composable, testable agent pipelines — and it's becoming the default for anyone shipping agents to production.

title: 20+ Prompts That Actually Work in 2026 — Tested Templates with Copy-Paste Examples
description: A Reddit roundup of 20+ battle-tested prompt templates spanning research, coding, content creation, and analysis has hit the front page of r/PromptEngineering. Each template includes fill-in-the-blank variables and real-world output examples, making it one of the most actionable prompt collections this year.

title: 1,446 Trending AI Image Prompts for GPT Image 2 & NanoBanana — Fully Open Source
description: A community-built open-source repository of 1,446 viral image prompts sourced from X trends has been released on r/PromptEngineering. The collection includes system prompts, MCP server integration, and ranked-by-likes organization — a goldmine for anyone studying what makes image prompts actually perform in production.
